title: An Ontology-Driven MCP Agent Framework for Ensuring Integrity in Architectural Structural Design
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Large Language Model (LLM)-based autonomous agents has demonstrated strong potential for automating knowledge-intensive tasks. However, their probabilistic reasoning often leads to hallucinations and weak domain knowledge integrity, limiting their applicability in high-stakes architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) contexts where engineering reliability is critical. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel architecture, termed the Ontology-driven Model Context Protocol (O-MCP) Framework, which integrates formal ontology with the standardized Model Context Protocol to ensure logical consistency and trustworthy knowledge exchange. The framework introduces a three-layer architecture consisting of a Knowledge and Data Source Layer, a Semantic MCP Server Layer, and an Intelligent Agent Layer. Its core contribution is the Semantic MCP Server, which incorporates a semantic guardrail mechanism that validates agent requests in real time using ontology-based reasoning. A case study in architectural structural design illustrates how the framework formalizes expert tacit knowledge and preserves decentralized knowledge sovereignty. A proof-of-concept experiment in reinforced concrete slab design positions O-MCP as a reference standard for formalized expert knowledge.
pdfFile: Paik et al. - 2026 - An Ontology-Driven MCP Agent Framework for Ensuring Integrity in Architectural Structural Design
markdownFile: Paik et al. - 2026 - An Ontology-Driven MCP Agent Framework for Ensuring Integrity in Architectural Structural Design
task: 建筑结构设计知识完整性保障
taskEn: Ensuring knowledge integrity in architectural structural design
inputData: 钢筋混凝土楼板设计参数、形式化本体知识、设计规范
inputDataEn: Reinforced concrete slab design parameters, formal ontology knowledge, design codes
contribution: 提出O-MCP框架，将形式化本体与标准化模型上下文协议（MCP）集成，通过语义护栏机制实时验证Agent请求，确保建筑结构设计中的知识完整性与逻辑一致性，为AEC领域工程可靠性提供参考标准。
methodology: 三层架构框架：(1)知识与数据源层——存储形式化本体（OWL）和领域数据；(2)语义MCP服务器层——核心贡献，集成语义护栏机制，基于本体推理实时验证Agent请求的逻辑一致性与知识完整性；(3)智能Agent层——基于Claude 3.5 Sonnet的LLM Agent执行结构设计任务。概念验证实验以钢筋混凝土楼板设计为案例，30个测试用例对比LLM RAG系统与O-MCP框架。
results: LLM RAG系统达到约83%的与形式化本体一致性，但在数值推理和多条件规则应用中存在系统性错误。O-MCP框架通过本体验证提供确定性的正确性保障，消除了RAG系统中的数值推理错误和多条件规则应用偏差，确保工程完整性。
